{"key_event":"Constitutional checks and post-1968 civic mobilization","reasoning":"France retained an exceptionally capable centralized state through Fifth Republic executive dominance, prefectural administration, and state-led modernization, but the oil shock, media liberalization, and stronger constitutional review slightly reduced unchecked state authority. Civil society rose further as voters forced de Gaulle out, the left rebuilt into a credible opposition, rights movements expanded, and constitutional/privacy protections gave associations and parliamentary minorities stronger tools to constrain the state.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":9.1,"state_change":-0.1,"state_power":9.0}
